title: Video jobs scale differently from still images
user_phrasing: why does video fail when images work
canonical_summary: Video pipelines often fail at frame counts or batch sizes that still-image workflows handle easily because temporal workloads amplify VRAM pressure.
source_priority_rule: Video and VRAM guidance outrank image-only instincts.
answer_pattern: Explain that temporal workloads change memory behavior.
exactness_guard: Do not treat video like a still-image job.

title: Frame count is a memory multiplier
canonical_summary: Longer frame counts often multiply memory pressure and runtime exposure, especially in video models.
source_priority_rule: Current lane guidance outranks general assumptions.
answer_pattern: Explain the frame-count effect and recommend testing shorter runs.
exactness_guard: Do not imply frame count is a cosmetic choice only.

title: Batching and caching matter
user_phrasing: what does frames per batch do
why does cache hurt video jobs
canonical_summary: Frames per batch, caching behavior, and offload settings can determine whether a video run fits in memory.
source_priority_rule: Runtime and workflow docs outrank guesswork.
answer_pattern: Point to batching/cache knobs as first-line memory controls.
exactness_guard: Do not overfocus on prompt wording for OOM-class failures.

title: Offload and tiling are tradeoffs
user_phrasing: what helps fit it in memory
canonical_summary: Offload, tiled decode, and lower memory modes can help jobs fit, but they may trade speed or quality.
source_priority_rule: VRAM strategy notes outrank simplistic quality assumptions.
exactness_guard: Do not promise free performance improvements.

title: Out of memory during longer runs
canonical_summary: OOM during longer video runs strongly suggests frame count, batch size, or cache behavior needs adjustment.
source_priority_rule: Runtime logs and lane guidance outrank intuition.
answer_pattern: Route to memory tuning and test smaller frame counts first.
exactness_guard: Do not send OOM errors to unrelated lanes.

title: Progressive slowdown and memory creep
canonical_summary: Progressive slowdown across repeated runs may indicate cache buildup, retained memory, or failure to reset between video jobs.
source_priority_rule: Observed run behavior outranks single-run assumptions.
answer_pattern: Identify possible cache/retention behavior and recommend reset or cache-clearing steps.
exactness_guard: Do not claim the cause is only frame count if memory creep is the symptom.

title: Short clip works, long clip fails
canonical_summary: A clip that works at short length and fails at longer length is a classic scaling signature for VRAM pressure.
source_priority_rule: Scaling behavior is a strong routing signal.
answer_pattern: Call out the scale-change signature and recommend stepwise expansion.
exactness_guard: Do not suggest a complete reinstall first.

title: VRAM issue vs runtime install issue
user_phrasing: is this vram or cuda setup
canonical_summary: If the error is a direct OOM or scaling failure, route here. If the model never starts because of import/runtime errors, route to runtime alignment.
source_priority_rule: Exact error text drives the branch.
answer_pattern: Differentiate memory pressure from runtime setup failure.
exactness_guard: Do not merge install failures into memory tuning.

title: VRAM issue vs bad model expectation
user_phrasing: can my card even run this
is the model too big or am I just tuning wrong
canonical_summary: If repeated low-frame tests fail immediately, the model or precision mode may be too large. If short tests work, optimize memory before abandoning the setup.
source_priority_rule: Observed behavior and model requirements drive this branch.
answer_pattern: Use short-run sanity tests to decide whether tuning or model choice is the primary issue.
exactness_guard: Do not tell the user to buy new hardware before simpler checks.

title: Trust runtime evidence for memory claims
user_phrasing: what source matters most for vram issues
canonical_summary: For memory issues, runtime logs and measured behavior outrank memory-based advice and old tuning notes.
answer_pattern: State that observed runtime behavior should lead the diagnosis.
exactness_guard: Do not overrule live evidence with stale lore.

title: Do not confuse VRAM pressure with prompt failure
user_phrasing: the output is bad so maybe that's why it crashed
is this just a bad prompt
canonical_summary: Prompt quality can affect usefulness, but OOM, scaling failures, and progressive memory creep are primarily memory-management problems.
answer_pattern: Separate memory failure diagnosis from creative prompt tuning.
exactness_guard: Do not redirect memory errors into prompt editing.

title: How to answer video VRAM questions
user_phrasing: what is the right answer shape for video vram issues
canonical_summary: Responses should identify the scaling symptom, recommend the smallest safe test, and point to the most likely memory controls.
source_priority_rule: Answer pattern guidance shapes the response.
answer_pattern: Use: identify symptom -> shrink test -> adjust one memory control at a time.
exactness_guard: Do not recommend too many simultaneous changes at once.
